own_review.issue: ThreadLocal IS_FIRST leak: IS_FIRST.set(false) executes before the scan, but IS_FIRST.remove() sits inside 'if (subFolders != null)' and is not in a finally block. If dir.listFiles() returns null (I/O/permission error) or locatePomReference throws mid-loop, the ThreadLocal stays false forever on that thread and ALL subsequent aggregator directories are silently skipped. Needs try/finally.
Depends on unreleased io.takari.polyglot:polyglot-common:0.4.9-SNAPSHOT (requires unmerged takari/polyglot-maven#242). Build is not reproducible and the PR cannot be merged/released as-is.

Behavior regression: nested aggregator directories (e.g. 'releng/plugins') were previously auto-discovered via COMMON_NAMES recursion; now only directly nested modules are detected ('Skip nested folder' branch). Also, the removed tycho.pomless.aggregator.names system property is now silently ignored for users who set it. Both deserve a release-note/migration mention.

assertMentionedDirectories uses assertEquals against an ordered list (['target','otherFolder','target']) derived from File.listFiles(), whose order is unspecified by the JDK; potentially flaky across filesystems/platforms.

testPomlessTestPluginDetection weakened from 'clean test' to 'clean compile' (unrelated to this PR's purpose); the dead verifyTextInLog("") removal is fine, but the goal change reduces coverage of test-plugin execution.

isValidLocation switched from String.endsWith to Path.of(location).endsWith — semantic change from suffix match (would match 'myfeature.xml') to exact last-path-element match. This is stricter/more correct, but inconsistent with TychoTargetMapping/TychoRepositoryMapping which still use String.endsWith.

points.point: ThreadLocal state leak: IS_FIRST.remove() inside if(subFolders!=null), set(false) already executed, permanent skip on that thread; fix with try/finally
points.note: Verified against the diff: remove() is only reached when subFolders != null and no exception occurs. This is the key correctness bug of the new mechanism.

points.point: ThreadLocal persists across Maven builds / IDE daemon, 'never re-initialized'
points.note: Overstated: remove() does reset it on the normal path; persistence only matters after the leak in point 1, which it duplicates.

points.point: IS_FIRST does not enforce 'directly nested' semantics; scenario of a bundle with stale pom.tycho causing the first real aggregator to be skipped
points.note: The conceptual criticism (thread-first != directly-nested) has merit, but the concrete scenario is incorrect: any top-level call ends with remove(), so absent the point-1 leak the next directory still scans.

points.point: Path.of(location).endsWith(TYCHO_POM) stricter than String.endsWith — confirm intentional
points.note: Verified real semantic change (substring vs last path element).

points.point: Same Path-based matching change for FEATURE_XML
Removed default getPriority(); verified all subclasses override it
points.note: Descriptive, no issue raised; also omits that AbstractXMLTychoMapping already defines priority 1.

points.point: Unrelated test scope change: 'clean test' -> 'clean compile' in testPomlessTestPluginDetection

missed: Human's insight: TychoFeatureMapping priority must beat polyglot's XmlMapping because MappingSupport.accept() accepts any *.xml location — previously relied on plexus sort order (implementation detail)
Behavior regression: nested aggregator-in-aggregator directories no longer discovered; tycho.pomless.aggregator.names silently ignored for users who configured it
Flaky ordered assertEquals on File.listFiles()-dependent skip-log order in PomlessTest
exceeds: ThreadLocal leak via remove() outside finally / subFolders==null path — a verified real bug not mentioned by the human reviewer and the sharpest finding on this PR

points.point: IS_FIRST mechanism 'broken': remove() resets supplier so every aggregator scans again; 'Skip nested folder' else branch essentially unreachable
points.note: False. Verified: during the scan loop, locatePomReference(subfolder) re-enters TychoAggregatorMapping.getPrimaryArtifact while IS_FIRST==false, so the else branch IS reached for every pom-less subfolder; remove() afterwards intentionally re-arms it for the next top-level aggregator. The mechanism works as designed (modulo the leak v6 never identifies).

points.point: Skip messages never emitted during scan; test assertion at PomlessTest.java:143 cannot pass; fix by logging inside the for-loop
points.note: False, same misunderstanding. The skip messages come exactly from the nested re-entrant calls; the IT assertions match real log output (verifier always runs with -X, so debug lines are present).

points.point: Early return on existing current pom.tycho skips IS_FIRST evaluation, 'creating inconsistent state'
points.note: False consequence: that path mutates no state, so no inconsistency arises; the observation describes intended caching behavior.

points.point: ThreadLocal across thread-pool reuse is a risk, though pattern 'resets rather than pollutes'; complexity concern
points.note: Vague hedged concern; concedes there is no pollution; padded with irrelevant 'code-explorer' detail.

points.point: Path.of(location).endsWith(FEATURE_XML) behavior change; Windows backslash caveat; 'keep String.endsWith for consistency with TychoAggregatorMapping's Path.of usage'
points.note: Core observation correct but the recommendation is self-contradictory (the aggregator also uses Path.of, so Path.of IS the consistent choice) and the Windows caveat is muddled.

points.point: maven.config property removal is correct given the whitelist is gone
TYCHO_POMLESS_AGGREGATOR_NAMES_PROPERTY constant removed; grep to ensure no other references
points.note: Checklist suggestion; verified there are no other code references, so nothing actionable.

points.point: polyglot-common 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT blocks merge until takari/polyglot-maven#242 is released
points.note: Verified merge blocker, correctly tied to the upstream PR.

missed: The real ThreadLocal bug: IS_FIRST.remove() not in finally and skipped when listFiles() returns null or an exception is thrown -> permanent silent skip (v6 discussed the ThreadLocal at length but mischaracterized it instead of finding the actual defect)
Human's insight: feature mapping priority must beat polyglot's XmlMapping which accepts any *.xml location
Behavior regression: nested aggregator directories no longer discovered; removed system property silently ignored
Flaky ordered assertEquals on File.listFiles()-dependent skip-log order
Unrelated weakening of testPomlessTestPluginDetection from 'test' to 'compile'

qualitative: v5 delivered a genuinely strong review: it found the one real code bug in the PR (the IS_FIRST.remove() leak path when listFiles() returns null or the loop throws), correctly flagged the unreleasable 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT dependency, and added accurate secondary observations on the Path.endsWith semantic change and the unrelated test-goal weakening, with only mild overreach in its two speculative ThreadLocal follow-ups. v6 anchored its entire review on a fabricated claim that the IS_FIRST mechanism is 'broken' and the skip branch 'unreachable' — verification of locatePomReference shows the aggregator mapping re-enters itself for each subfolder while the flag is false, so the mechanism and the new IT assertions work exactly as intended; two of its points are absence-fabrications (claiming skip logs are never emitted and tests cannot pass) and a third invents a non-existent 'inconsistent state'. Neither candidate reproduced the human's deeper insight about polyglot's XmlMapping accepting any *.xml location, nor the nested-aggregator behavior regression. Despite v6 having extra git tooling, v5 is decisively better: it verified behavior before asserting, while v6 confidently reasoned itself into false blockers.
